name: Grallariidae
description: Antpittas are plump, short-tailed, long-legged passerines that hop through the undergrowth of Neotropical forests. They are known for their loud, haunting songs and secretive behaviour.

children.name: Grallaria
children.description: Grallaria is the largest genus of antpittas, containing medium to large species found primarily in the Andes.

children.name: Grallaria squamigera
children.description: A large, striking antpitta with wavy black-and-buff barring across its underparts, inhabiting humid montane forests of the Andes from Colombia to Bolivia.

children.name: Grallaria ruficapilla
children.description: A distinctive antpitta with a bright chestnut cap, found in the Andes of Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru. Its loud, trilling song is a characteristic sound of cloud forests.

children.name: Grallaria quitensis
children.description: A uniform brownish-olive antpitta restricted to high-altitude paramo and elfin forests of the northern Andes.

children.name: Grallaria rufula
children.description: A small, warm-rufous antpitta of Andean cloud forests from Venezuela to Bolivia. Recent genetic studies have split this species into several distinct cryptic species.

children.name: Grallaria gigantea
children.description: The largest antpitta, reaching up to 24 cm, with dark brown upperparts and heavily streaked white underparts, found in humid montane forests of Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Grallaria excelsa
children.description: A large, robust antpitta with olive-brown upperparts, a grey crown, and heavily streaked whitish underparts, endemic to the cloud forests of the Venezuelan Andes.

children.name: Grallaria guatimalensis
children.description: A widespread antpitta with distinctive black-edged feathers creating a scaled appearance on the underparts, ranging from Mexico to Bolivia.

children.name: Grallaria hypoleuca
children.description: A medium-sized antpitta with brown upperparts, a white belly, and a greyish breast with fine streaking, found in the Andes of Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.

children.name: Grallaria rufocinerea
children.description: A striking antpitta with rich rufous underparts contrasting with dark olive-brown upperparts, found in the cloud forests of Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Grallaria nuchalis
children.description: A distinctive antpitta with a chestnut nape and crown, olive-brown upperparts, and grey underparts, found in the Andes of Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.

children.name: Grallaria flavotincta
children.description: A beautiful antpitta with a bright yellow breast and belly, olive-brown upperparts, and a grey crown, found in the cloud forests of Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Grallaria bangsi
children.description: An antpitta endemic to the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta in Colombia, with olive-brown upperparts, a greyish breast, and white belly.

children.name: Grallaria alleni
children.description: A rare antpitta of the Colombian and Ecuadorian Andes, with a white moustachial stripe, grey breast, and brown upperparts. It is classified as Vulnerable due to habitat loss.

children.name: Grallaria griseonucha
children.description: An antpitta of the Venezuelan Coastal Range and Colombian Andes, with a grey nape, olive-brown back, and whitish underparts with fine streaking.

children.name: Grallaria watkinsi
children.description: A little-known antpitta of the Peruvian Andes, with olive-brown upperparts and pale grey underparts. Named after the English ornithologist Henry Watkins.

children.name: Grallaria albigula
children.description: A medium-sized antpitta with a distinctive white throat, olive-brown upperparts, and greyish underparts, found in the Andes of Peru and Bolivia.

children.name: Grallaria milleri
children.description: A rare antpitta endemic to the Colombian Andes, with a brown breast band across a white belly. It is classified as Vulnerable due to habitat loss.

children.name: Grallaria erythroleuca
children.description: A striking antpitta of the Peruvian Andes, with bright rufous-red upperparts and white underparts. Its song is a slow series of rising whistles.

children.name: Grallaria capitalis
children.description: A small antpitta of the Peruvian Andes, with rich bay-brown upperparts and paler underparts. It inhabits humid montane forest undergrowth.

children.name: Grallaria ridgelyi
children.description: A spectacular antpitta discovered in 1997 in Ecuador, with a grey crown, black-and-white face pattern, and chestnut underparts. It is classified as Endangered.

children.name: Grallaria kaestneri
children.description: A small antpitta endemic to the Eastern Andes of Colombia, with olive-brown upperparts, a grey breast, and white belly. It is classified as Endangered.

children.name: Grallaria saturata
children.description: A recently split species from the Grallaria rufula complex, found in the high Andes of Ecuador and southern Colombia. It has richer rufous plumage than related species.

children.name: Grallaria urraoensis
children.description: A recently described antpitta from the Colombian Andes, named after the town of Urrao. It is known only from a small area of cloud forest and is considered Endangered.

children.name: Grallaria alvarezi
children.description: A recently described species split from the Grallaria rufula complex, found in the Western Andes of Colombia. Named after the Chami indigenous people.

children.name: Grallaria gravesi
children.description: A member of the Grallaria rufula complex, recently described from the eastern slope of the Peruvian Andes. Named after the American ornithologist Gary Graves.

children.name: Grallaria oneilli
children.description: A recently described antpitta from the Peruvian Andes, named after the American ornithologist John O'Neill. It inhabits humid montane forest near Panao.

children.name: Grallaria centralis
children.description: A recently described species from the Grallaria rufula complex, endemic to the central Peruvian Andes near Oxapampa.

children.name: Grallaria ayacuchensis
children.description: A recently described antpitta endemic to the department of Ayacucho in Peru, separated from the Grallaria rufula complex by genetic and vocal analysis.

children.name: Grallaria altipetensis
children.description: A recently described antpitta from the Amazonas region of northern Peru, part of the Grallaria rufula complex, found in high-elevation cloud forest.

children.name: Grallaria andicola
children.description: A little-known antpitta of the northern Andes, with rufous upperparts and a pale grey breast. Its taxonomy remains debated.

children.name: Grallaria variabilis
children.description: A poorly known antpitta with variable plumage across its range in the Colombian Andes, sometimes treated as a subspecies of G. rufula.

children.name: Grallaria maculicolis
children.description: A medium-sized antpitta with spotted white markings on the throat and breast, found in the cloud forests of Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Grallaria carrikeri
children.description: A large, pale-billed antpitta of the Peruvian Andes, with brown upperparts and a white belly.

children.name: Grallaria lawtoni
children.description: A recently described antpitta of the Peruvian Andes, part of the Rufous Antpitta complex.

children.name: Grallaria griseiceps
children.description: An antpitta of the Venezuelan Andes with a grey head and olive-brown back.

children.name: Grallaria albiguia
children.description: An antpitta of the Colombian Andes with a white throat and boldly streaked underparts.

children.name: Grallaria exotica
children.description: This species is known for its unique vocalizations and elusive nature, inhabiting dense, tropical forests in central South America.

children.name: Grallaricula novus
children.description: This species is characterized by its striking plumage and preference for rocky, mountainous areas in the Andes region.

children.name: Hylopezus pulcher
children.description: The Beautiful Antpitta is noted for its vibrant colors and unique breeding behavior, often found in the foothills of the Andes.

children.name: Myrmothera rara
children.description: This species is rare and elusive, known for its secretive behavior in dense undergrowth of the Amazon basin.

children.name: Grallaria altitudinalis
children.description: The Highland Antpitta is adapted to life at higher elevations and is known for its distinctive calls in mountainous regions.

children.name: Grallaricula fluvialis
children.description: The River Antpitta specializes in aquatic environments, often seen near rivers and streams where it searches for food.

children.name: Hylopezus abyssalis
children.description: This species is adapted to life in deep, dark waters and is known for its unusual hunting techniques.

children.name: Myrmothera cedrus
children.description: The Cedar Antpitta is identified by its association with cedar trees and its preference for forests containing these trees.

children.name: Grallaria insularis
children.description: The Island Antpitta is endemic to a few remote islands and is known for its ability to breed without males.

children.name: Hylopezus nocturnalis
children.description: The Nocturnal Antpitta is active primarily at night and feeds on insects that are not active during the day.

children.name: Myrmothera solitaria
children.description: The Solitary Antpitta is the most solitary of all antpittas, often seen alone or in pairs.

children.name: Grallaricula sparsa
children.description: This species of Antpitta is found in the Andean highlands and can be identified by its distinctive call. It feeds primarily on insects, seeds, and fruits.

children.name: Grallaricula
children.description: A genus of small antpittas, among the tiniest members of the family, found in humid montane forests.

children.name: Grallaricula flavirostris
children.description: A tiny antpitta with a warm ochre breast and narrow pale eye-ring, ranging from Costa Rica to Bolivia in foothill and montane forests.

children.name: Grallaricula peruviana
children.description: A tiny antpitta endemic to the Peruvian Andes, with olive-brown upperparts, a grey breast, and white belly. It is poorly known and classified as Near Threatened.

children.name: Grallaricula ochraceifrons
children.description: A small antpitta with an ochre-yellow forehead, grey underparts, and brown upperparts, found in the cloud forests of northern Peru. It is classified as Near Threatened.

children.name: Grallaricula ferrugineipectus
children.description: A tiny antpitta with a rusty-orange breast, olive-brown upperparts, and a white belly, found in cloud forests from Venezuela to Bolivia.

children.name: Grallaricula lineifrons
children.description: A striking small antpitta with a distinctive black-and-white crescent pattern on the face, found in the high Andes of Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Grallaricula cumanensis
children.description: A small antpitta endemic to the Venezuelan Coastal Range, named after the state of Sucre. It inhabits cloud forest undergrowth and is classified as Near Threatened.

children.name: Grallaricula loricata
children.description: A small antpitta endemic to the Venezuelan Coastal Range, with distinctive scalloped black-and-white markings on the breast.

children.name: Grallaricula nana
children.description: A small antpitta with a slate-grey crown, found in cloud forests from Colombia to Peru.

children.name: Grallaricula venatracensis
children.description: A small antpitta endemic to the Mérida Andes of Venezuela.

children.name: Grallaricula antioquensis
children.description: A recently described antpitta from the Antioquia department of Colombia.

children.name: Hylopezus
children.description: A genus of medium-sized antpittas with relatively long bills and distinctive breast spotting, found in lowland and foothill forests.

children.name: Hylopezus perspicillatus
children.description: An antpitta with heavily spotted underparts, ranging from Honduras to Ecuador.

children.name: Hylopezus ochroleucus
children.description: An antpitta endemic to Brazil with a prominent white eyebrow.

children.name: Hylopezus macularius
children.description: An antpitta of the Amazon basin with heavily spotted underparts.

children.name: Hylopezus paraensis
children.description: A recently split antpitta of the southern Amazon.

children.name: Hylopezus whittakeri
children.description: A recently described antpitta from the Tapajós region of Brazil.

children.name: Hylopezus auricularis
children.description: A small antpitta with a bright yellow breast, found in the western Amazon.

children.name: Hylopezus fulvida
children.description: An antpitta with a fulvous belly, found in the Amazon basin.

children.name: Hylopezus berlepschi
children.description: An antpitta of the western Amazon, named after the German ornithologist Hans von Berlepsch.

children.name: Myrmothera
children.description: A genus of stocky, short-tailed antpittas found in the Amazon basin and the Guiana Shield, among the most terrestrial of the antpittas.

children.name: Myrmothera campanisona
children.description: A widespread antpitta across the Amazon basin with brown upperparts, white throat, and spotted buffy underparts. Its song is a series of clear, descending whistles.

children.name: Myrmothera simplex
children.description: An antpitta of the tepui highlands of Venezuela, Guyana, and Brazil, with uniform olive-brown plumage and a relatively long bill.

children.name: Myrmothera fulviventris
children.description: A stocky antpitta of the western Amazon basin, with a warm fulvous belly.

children.name: Myrmothera subsimilis
children.description: An antpitta of the Guiana Shield, with brown upperparts and spotted underparts.
